title: TC007-PR mode: Analyze a valid PR URL and render full PR report card sections
description: Verifies that a valid GitHub pull request URL can be analyzed and the PR report card, workflow recommendation, and preflight checklist are displayed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Click the "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode, enter the PR URL into the input, and submit via Enter.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Pull Request')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Pull Request' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'loading skeleton')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'loading skeleton' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'PRReportCard')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'PRReportCard' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Pre-flight Checklist')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Pre-flight Checklist' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Submit resulted in an error: 'Pull request not found or repository is private.' displayed after submitting the PR URL.
- PR report card not displayed after submission (expected PRReportCard element absent).
- Workflow recommendation not displayed after submission.
- 'Pre-flight Checklist' text not visible after analysis completed.

title: TC012-Analyze a valid GitHub issue URL and view the full IssueReportCard
description: Verifies a user can analyze an issue URL and see the IssueReportCard sections rendered.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Click the 'Issue' tab to switch to issue mode and enter the issue URL into the Repository URL input.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/button[3]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World/issues/1')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'loading skeleton')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'loading skeleton' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Issue Summary')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Issue Summary' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Work Status')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Work Status' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Final Recommendation')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Final Recommendation' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Submit action did not start analysis because error message 'That URL points to a pull request, not an issue. Use PR mode to analyze it.' is displayed.
- Loading skeleton was not rendered after submission; no progress indicator appeared.
- 'Issue Summary' section is not present on the page after submission.
- 'Work Status' section is not present on the page after submission.
- 'Final Recommendation' section is not present on the page after submission.

title: TC022-Immediately switching modes after submitting Issue analysis clears previous mode report area
description: Verifies that switching modes right after clicking Analyze does not leave Issue report content visible in the new mode.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Click the 'Issue' tab to switch to Issue mode (element index 58).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/button[3]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/1')
        
        # -> Click the 'Repo' tab to switch to Repo mode so assertions can be checked.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
